short_description:
Grupo Financiero Banorte is a prominent Mexican financial group that offers a wide range of banking and financial solutions. Known as "El Banco Fuerte de México" (The Strong Bank of Mexico), Banorte serves various customer profiles, including individual consumers and corporate clients.

In personal banking, Banorte provides services such as deposit accounts that can be opened remotely, payroll account services, credit cards including co-branded options, and general credit products. Their mobile banking app, Banorte Móvil, allows customers to manage their accounts and conduct transactions easily. For corporate clients, Banorte offers treasury services, liquidity management, and trade financing to support business operations. Additionally, the group provides specialized financial products like insurance and annuities through its subsidiaries.

Banorte prioritizes security and customer support, focusing on fraud prevention and data protection while offering dedicated assistance through a contact center for individual customers.
description: Segundo banco más grande de México. Amplia infraestructura VMware afectada por nuevos precios Broadcom.

short_description:
CEMEX S.A.B. de C.V. is a leading multinational building materials company based in San Pedro Garza García, Mexico. Founded in 1906, CEMEX has grown to become one of the world's largest cement producers, operating in over 50 countries. The company has a strong presence in the Americas, Europe, Africa, the Middle East, and Asia, and is recognized for its extensive portfolio of construction materials and solutions.

CEMEX specializes in manufacturing cement, ready-mix concrete, and aggregates, while also providing innovative urbanization solutions. The company emphasizes sustainability and circular economy practices, utilizing waste as alternative fuels and materials. CEMEX is committed to achieving carbon neutrality through research and development, alongside enhancing digital customer experiences. With a robust trading network and a focus on high-growth markets, CEMEX continues to meet the demands of the construction industry worldwide.

short_description:
BBVA México S.A. is a prominent financial institution and a subsidiary of Spain's Banco Bilbao Vizcaya Argentaria (BBVA). Established in 1932 as Banco de Comercio in Mexico City, it evolved into Bancomer and was rebranded as BBVA México in 2020. The bank has a rich history marked by significant milestones, including nationalization in 1982 and subsequent privatization in 1991, which restored private control. BBVA México has grown to become the largest bank in Mexico, serving approximately 18.3 million customers with a strong focus on innovation and financial inclusion.

The bank provides a wide range of services for individuals, families, and businesses. Its offerings include commercial banking, mortgages through Hipotecaria Nacional, pensions via Afore Bancomer, and insurance products from Seguros Bancomer and Wibe. BBVA México is known for its historical innovations, such as introducing Mexico's first credit cards and ATMs. Today, it emphasizes digital banking and structured products, while also engaging in corporate responsibility initiatives to support economic development.
